counterfactual_schedules.legality: Legal only for read-only/idempotent effects with no data dependency between them and acceptable freshness/cache policy.
counterfactual_schedules.name: parallel_read_only_effects
counterfactual_schedules.rewrite: Group independent read-only tool/retrieval calls after the planning boundary and execute them concurrently; return compact answer slices to the final model call.
counterfactual_schedules.risk: May increase provider load or rate-limit pressure; cap concurrency and preserve cancellation.
counterfactual_schedules.validation: Trace dependency reasons, compare final quality, monitor rate limits and tool failure fanout.

counterfactual_schedules.legality: Legal if intermediate natural language is not a required user-visible artifact and downstream calls consume only structured decisions.
counterfactual_schedules.name: collapse_serial_model_round_trips
counterfactual_schedules.rewrite: Replace serial planner/executor/formatter model calls with one structured response or a planner once plus deterministic/fast executor steps.
counterfactual_schedules.risk: Prompt complexity may rise; validate accuracy and parse stability.
counterfactual_schedules.validation: Golden eval tasks, parse-failure rate, model-request count per task, p95 final latency.

counterfactual_schedules.legality: Semantics unchanged if only prompt ordering changes and the final prompt content remains equivalent.
counterfactual_schedules.name: stable_prefix_cache_treaty
counterfactual_schedules.rewrite: Freeze instructions, examples, tool schemas, and structured output schema at the prompt prefix; move dynamic content later; set prompt_cache_key by route/prefix family.
counterfactual_schedules.risk: Accidental prompt behavior change from ordering; eval before rollout.
counterfactual_schedules.validation: cached_tokens/input_tokens by route, answer quality eval, p95 prefill latency.

counterfactual_schedules.legality: Legal if the downstream model only requires the slice; raw payload can remain available outside the model path for audit/drilldown.
counterfactual_schedules.name: provider_answer_slice
counterfactual_schedules.rewrite: Change tools/retrievers to return bounded answer slices with ids/citations/confidence instead of raw documents or full payloads in the model path.
counterfactual_schedules.risk: Could omit relevant context; use retrieval eval and fallback expansion.
counterfactual_schedules.validation: Answer quality, omitted-context error rate, tool result token budget, fallback expansion rate.

findings.expected_savings: one or more model round trips if semantic dependencies are accidental
proof_needed: verify second call consumes unique output from first
findings.rewrite: Combine steps into one structured call, or split once into planner plus deterministic/fast executor only when the first call produces a consumed plan.
findings.title: Consecutive model calls may be collapsible or routable

findings.expected_savings: up to 720ms observed duplicate tool time
findings.rewrite: Memoize/journal the first result inside the run; for durable routes, add freshness and replay policy before reusing across runs.
findings.title: Repeated equivalent tool call: web_search

findings.rewrite: Execute independent read-only tool calls concurrently, or issue them as a batch provider offer; preserve ordering only for data/authority/freshness edges.
findings.title: Read-only tools appear serialized

findings.expected_savings: input-token/prefill reduction and often fewer downstream summarization calls
findings.rewrite: Change the provider contract to return a bounded answer slice: facts, citations/ids, confidence, and omitted-result count. Keep raw payload outside the model path.
findings.title: Large tool result sent through agent loop: retrieval:web_search

findings.expected_savings: input-token/prefill reduction and often fewer downstream summarization calls
findings.rewrite: Change the provider contract to return a bounded answer slice: facts, citations/ids, confidence, and omitted-result count. Keep raw payload outside the model path.
findings.title: Large tool result sent through agent loop: retrieval:web_search

findings.expected_savings: lower prefill latency/cost for repeated long-prefix routes
findings.rewrite: Move static instructions/examples/tools/schemas to the exact prefix, move dynamic history/RAG/user data later, stabilize JSON/tool ordering, and set a route-appropriate prompt_cache_key.
findings.title: Long model prompt has poor cache reuse: planner (o4-mini)

findings.expected_savings: lower prefill latency/cost for repeated long-prefix routes
findings.rewrite: Move static instructions/examples/tools/schemas to the exact prefix, move dynamic history/RAG/user data later, stabilize JSON/tool ordering, and set a route-appropriate prompt_cache_key.
findings.title: Long model prompt has poor cache reuse: synthesize_tool_results (gpt-5.5)

findings.expected_savings: lower prefill latency/cost for repeated long-prefix routes
findings.rewrite: Move static instructions/examples/tools/schemas to the exact prefix, move dynamic history/RAG/user data later, stabilize JSON/tool ordering, and set a route-appropriate prompt_cache_key.
findings.title: Long model prompt has poor cache reuse: format_final_answer (gpt-5.5)

findings.expected_savings: decode latency roughly scales with generated tokens; TTFU improves with streaming
findings.rewrite: Use a shorter answer contract, structured fields, max output budget, or progressive rendering. If the long output is necessary, stream safe partial content.
findings.title: Large model output likely dominates decode latency: synthesize_tool_results (gpt-5.5)

Route bounded execution to faster GPT workhorse; reserve reasoning model for planner
Stream safe partial output to improve TTFU
operations.rewrite_legality: On observed critical path; rewrite requires preserving data, safety, authority, freshness, and quality semantics.

patch_plan: Add per-run tool-result memoization keyed by tool name, normalized args hash, freshness policy, and authority scope.
Introduce an async tool batch executor for read-only/idempotent effects; require dependency reasons for serial edges.
Refactor planner/executor sequence into one structured model response or a planner-once plus deterministic executor path.
Refactor prompt builder into static_prefix + dynamic_suffix; sort JSON/tool schemas deterministically; set prompt_cache_key by prefix family.
Modify providers to return compact answer slices and keep raw payloads in external storage by id.
Enable streaming on long-generation routes and add a safe progress/partial-output UI path.
Ship behind a feature flag; compare old/new routes on golden traces and production canary.
validation_plan: Replay golden traces and compare final outputs, tool outputs, and safety decisions.
Track p50/p95/p99 end-to-end latency, TTFU, model request count, tool call count, serial depth, and idle gaps.
Track input, cached, output, reasoning, retrieval, and tool-result tokens by route.
Add a CI gate: no new serial model round trip on the dominant route without an explicit treaty blocker.
Add cache-hit dashboard: cached_tokens / input_tokens by prompt prefix and prompt_cache_key.
Add answer-slice retrieval eval: compact tool output must preserve answer quality and citations/ids.
A/B concurrency limits and monitor rate limits, provider errors, and tail latency.
verdict: Highest-leverage observed issue: Consecutive model calls may be collapsible or routable (ROUND_TRIP_AMPLIFICATION). Rewrite: Combine steps into one structured call, or split once into planner plus deterministic/fast executor only when the first call produces a consumed plan.
